_instructions:
You are helping enrich a Metaphysical Bible Dictionary (MIB) with Strong's concordance numbers.

For each entry in the "entries" array:
- "word" is the biblical word or concept
- "etym" is its original language (Heb. = Hebrew, Gk. = Greek)
- "mibDef" is its literal definition
- "mibMeta" is its metaphysical/spiritual interpretation (may be truncated)
- "candidates" are Strong's numbers suggested by a word-map lookup — these are hints ONLY, not exhaustive

Your task:
1. Using your knowledge of Hebrew and Greek Strong's concordance, identify ALL Strong's numbers
   whose meaning semantically matches the spiritual/metaphysical sense of this word.
2. The candidates list is a starting point — you may confirm some, discard others, and ADD
   additional Strong's numbers not in the candidates list if they genuinely match.
3. Use "etym" to stay in the right language (Heb. → H numbers, Gk. → G numbers) unless
   the concept clearly spans both Testaments.
4. Exclude unrelated homonyms (e.g. H7043 "light/trivial in weight" does NOT match
   "light" as divine illumination).

Return ONLY a valid JSON array — no explanation, no markdown, no code fences:
[
  { "id": <same id from input>, "matches": ["H216", "H215", "G5457"] },
  { "id": <same id from input>, "matches": [] },
  ...
]

Every entry in the input must appear in your output. Empty "matches" is valid if nothing fits.

entries.mibDef: oak trees; terebinth trees; grove of strong trees; palm grove; aggregation
entries.mibMeta: A strengthening of the whole nervous system (aggregation of strength, oak trees). A tree is a connecting link between the heavens and the earth, the formless and the formed. The nervous system in man connects the spiritual and the physical; it is in very close union with the mental thoughts,

candidates.def: Eloth or Elath, a place on the Red Sea

entries.mibDef: whom God has called; invocation of God; knowledge of God; God of
entries.mibMeta: A central thought that, although it belongs to the sense phase of man's consciousness, responds in measure to the quickening presence of Spirit (whom God has called). It perceives that God is the source of understanding (God of understanding, wisdom of God) yet it does not bring forth definite fruit

entries.mibDef: whom God loveth; loved of God; stirred by God
entries.mibMeta: The love of God, or the perception of God as love. Belief in law (Moses) in its apparently hard aspect of cause and effect, and its outer "thou shalt," and "thou shalt not," is not sufficient to meet the needs of our thought people. A knowledge of God as love is necessary, that a right adjustment ma

entries.mibDef: whom God puts on, i.e., fills and dons with Himself; whom God
entries.mibMeta: The individual will (Ephraim signifies the will) fully unified with and expressing the divine, having the quality of Godlikeness, and being eternal even as God is eternal. (The name Eleadah is prophetic, as many of the names of the Old Testament are, inasmuch as the Truth for which it stands was not

entries.mibDef: whither God ascends; whereto God arises; ascent of God; God is
entries.mibMeta: A state of consciousness that, though of Moab (carnal mind), exalts God, Spirit, Truth, and so passes into the possession of the Reubenites, who signify discernment, sight, faith. This state of consciousness is thus reestablished on a higher basis. It is the seemingly good in the carnal mind that ca

entries.mibDef: God is maker; whom God has made; God is creator.
entries.mibMeta: The recognition by the individual that God, Spirit, is the source of deliverance, strength, healing, and of true greatness in both the inner and the outer man (God is maker, whom God has made, God is creator). Helez signifies deliverance and strength through praise (Judah). Raphah gives the thought

candidates.def: Elasah, the name of four Israelites

entries.mibDef: God has surrounded; God succors; God is helper; whom God has
entries.mibMeta: Spiritual strength through the individual's recognition of God as his supporting, sustaining power ( God is helper). This becomes the presiding, directive faculty of a new state of consciousness. (See AARON.)

Eleazar, an Israelite

entries.mibDef: a "bondservant" (see Galatians 4:1,3)— but is getting hold of the idea of sonship

entries.mibDef: union; power; strength; instruction; learning; one thousand, i. e. , a very
entries.mibMeta: Union with infinite strength, through understanding and faith. (An ox stands for physical strength; Benjamin relates to an active faith; learning bespeaks understanding.)

entries.mibDef: God is gracious; grace of God; whom God has bestowed;
entries.mibMeta: A gracious, kindly, loving attitude of mind that emanates from God, Spirit, and is established in substance (Beth-lehem). This gives courage and fearlessness in combating seeming error.

candidates.def: Elchanan, an Israelite

entries.mibMeta: The intellect under spiritual discipline. Our first unfoldment is always through the intellect. We get an intellectual concept of Truth and in due season clothe it with substance and life. While Eli (meaning going up, ascent) is typical of the phase of man's consciousness that is always seeking spir

entries.mibDef: God is Father; strength of the Father.
entries.mibMeta: That in our religious and spiritual nature which takes cognizance of the Author of being (God is Father) as the source of strength (strength of the Father). entries.mibMeta: Ian's awakening, through the consciousness of love and active faith (David and Benjamin), to the understanding that God, Divine Mind, is the source of all knowing, of all knowledge. The loving (David) thought in this awakening bespeaks the idea that God cares, as well as knows.

candidates.def: Eljada, the name of two Israelites and of an Aramaean leader

entries.mibMeta: Our true thoughts are God's people. They are gathered together and established in consciousness by divine light and power.

entries.mibDef: God has come; God is come; to whom God comes.
entries.mibMeta: A realization and expression of the presence of God as divine harmony ( God is come; a musician or singer).

entries.mibDef: God is love; God loves; loved of God; God's beloved; God nourishes.
entries.mibMeta: A keen discrimination and insight into Truth, and into man's relation to the various phases of Truth. entries.mibMeta: The turning of the attention from the outer world of manifestation to Jehovah, the Christ, or Father within one, as the power that delivers from sense bondage and saves to the uttermost— brings again to the Promised Land.

candidates.def: Elijah, the name of the famous prophet and of two other Israelites

entries.mibDef: God is rejecter; God his rejecter; rejected of God.
entries.mibMeta: Elimination of fear (a Harodite is a native of Harod, or one belonging to Harod, and Harod means fear, terror , trembling). It is fear that is rejected of God, or in other words is eliminated by Truth. elimination— (see renunciation)

entries.mibDef: terebinths; palm trees; trees; oaks.
entries.mibMeta: A realization of fullness of life, strength, and cleansing Truth for the whole man; also a realization of victory (twelve is a number representing fullness in the spiritual. Springs of water stand for life and cleansing; oaks signify strength and protection; and palm trees denote victory).

entries.mibMeta: The consciousness of the soul (Naomi) that God is king. (See NAOMI and CHILION.)

to Jah are mine eyes; toward Jehovah are my eyes.

"Look unto me, and be ye saved, all the ends of the earth; for I am God, and there is none else" (Isa. 45:22). "Thou wilt keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on thee" (Isa. 26:3).

entries.mibMeta: He who is consecrated to God in thought and act is in turn exalted by God, is distinguished above his fellows. entries.mibDef: God is deliverance; God is
entries.mibMeta: Deliverance from error and its results— salvation— is from God; it is accomplished by the power of Spirit. It is not by personal might or power that we are enabled to obtain the good that is our divine birthright, "but by my Spirit, saith Jehovah of hosts" (Zech. 4:6).

candidates.def: Eliphelet or Elpelet, the name of six Israelites

entries.mibMeta: Spiritual judgment and adjustment active in the inner life force of man's organism (the kingdom of Judah represents the inner life force, or vitality).

entries.mibMeta: The full assurance in the soul or feminine, love consciousness, that God is a God of Truth and keeps His promises to man; in other words, the inner conviction of the soul that the working out of the law of life and Truth in man's consciousness is inevitable and sure.

entries.mibMeta: The understanding of love (David) that true riches are from Spirit; also that deliverance from all error, and help of every kind that pertains to salvation, are from the same source. (See ELISHAMA.)

entries.mibMeta: Praise and exaltation of God, Spirit, as being in authority, as being King of Kings and Lord of Lords. This attitude of mind is necessary that the Christ may be born into one's consciousness.

Reuben is faith in its aspect of discernment, sight; Shedeur, father of Elizur, means shedder of light.)

entries.mibDef: whom God has founded; God has created; possessed of God;
entries.mibMeta: The understanding by man that he is from God, that his inheritance is from God, that he does not selfishly own anything, but that God possesses all and all that the Father has is man's.

candidates.def: Elkanah, the name of several Israelites

entries.mibDef: bowed of God; my bow is God; inclined of God.
entries.mibMeta: My defense, might, power, are of God, Spirit (m bow is God; a bow represents the power of the will to act and to direct the consciousness). My willingness to do God's will is also the result of the activity of Spirit in my consciousness ( inclined of God).

entries.mibDef: strong rebellion; oath of Assyria; oak of Assyria.
entries.mibMeta: A state of consciousness whose central, controlling thought and belief is that for which Arioch stands. (See ARIOCH.) It does not look to Spirit for its strength and power, but trusts in "the mind of the flesh."

entries.mibDef: God's measure; power of extension;
entries.mibMeta: The enlarging of the consciousness so that it may comprehend something of the immeasurable greatness of God.

entries.mibDef: GW is pleasant; sweetness of God; delight of God; beauty of God;
entries.mibMeta: The spiritual joy, satisfaction, delight, and fair-minded judgment that adhere to the will ruling in love (David). These qualities of mind aid in supporting and protecting the ruling love idea in consciousness and in causing it to be victorious over opposing sense thoughts and activities.

entries.mibDef: bestowed of God; God gives; given of God; God's gift, i.e.,
entries.mibMeta: The conscience that God bestows- the inner intuition, discrimination, and sense of judgment that are given of God to man to enable him to know the right course to pursue and also to know the result of willfulness and disobedience. entries.mibMeta: Thoughts of strength and power. In the case of Elon the Hittite, the thought of strength and power is very material and is not enduring. In the significance of Elon who was a judge of Israel, and the city of Elon that belonged to the Israelitish tribe of Dan, the quality of judgment is also present.

entries.mibDef: observance of God; reverence of God; fear of God;
entries.mibMeta: Judgment (signified by Dan) is often looked upon by the outer man as punishment sent to him by a personal God because of his sins; thus he fears God (fear of God). As man unfolds, however, and begins to understand judgment and justice in a clearer light, he reverences divine law and ceases to fear.

entries.mibMeta: A place of fruition in consciousness (the gleaning month), also the earnest desire of the soul for greater fruitfulness (outcry).

In this way belief in a power of evil is established in consciousness and man fears and dreads man

entries.mibMeta: The first acceptance of the Christ, or love teaching of the heart, by the hardened, seemingly material, warring thoughts of the head (Rome), in individual consciousness. This is very praiseworthy and it causes great rejoicing in the realm of the word of Truth (Paul).

entries.mibDef: lovely; covered with foam, i. e. , impassioned.
entries.mibMeta: The loving message of the Christ (lovely) and its action in the Colossi and Rome states of mind. God is love and His expressions toward man are purely peaceful and good. entries.mibMeta: Thoughts on the animal plane of consciousness in man that are active, yet young and inexperienced (young hart, young deer, young calf). entries.mibMeta: Ephes-dammim bespeaks the end of warring thoughts and beliefs, the end of thoughts of strife and bloodshed, and the end of the error idea that life is material and limited ( end of bloodshed, limit of blood, boundary of blood). entries.mibDef: belonging to Ephesus.
entries.mibMeta: Thoughts that belong to the Ephesus state of consciousness in man. The Ephesians to whom the Epistle of Paul was written signify the enlightened and partially enlightened thoughts that belong to this state of consciousness. (See EPHESUS.)

entries.mibMeta: The central building faculty of the consciousness called desire. In its physical aspect Ephesus symbolizes the stomach.
